message.content: Scott, agreed on all points. The translation layer in the route handler is correctly structured. Deferring the `UserFacingError` type until we have more forms is the right call -- extract when there's duplication, not before. One issue I flagged to Luca: the template currently renders error elements conditionally (only when an error exists). They need to always be in the DOM with `role="alert"`, empty when no error. This ensures `aria-describedby` always resolves and screen readers announce errors when they appear after HTMX form resubmission. The translation layer on the Rust side is fine -- it's the template rendering that needs the fix. Also: the `DuplicateEmail` message uses inline HTML (`signing in`) inside a `role="alert"` div. This works -- Steve Schoger asked and I confirmed links inside alert regions are announced correctly. The hardcoded HTML in the Rust handler (not user-interpolated) avoids XSS. Just noting this so we don't accidentally introduce user input into that pattern later.

content.text: Good points from Heydon, though arriving late. The inline HTML for DuplicateEmail is handled via the `|safe` filter in the template. The always-in-DOM error elements point is a valid accessibility improvement we should address, but not right now -- we're in CI-gate mode and shouldn't write code until the team-lead says to proceed on Task #6.

input.content:
Heydon, two good points. Quick status: **1. Always-in-DOM error elements**: Valid accessibility concern. Currently the template renders error `

` elements conditionally with `{% if let Some(err) = email_error %}`. Your recommendation to always have them in the DOM (empty when no error) so `aria-describedby` always resolves is correct. This should be addressed as part of Task #6 or as a small follow-up. I'll flag it when we resume work. **2. Inline HTML in DuplicateEmail**: Noted. The template uses `{{ error|safe }}` for the general error to render the `` tag correctly. You're right that it's safe because it's hardcoded in the Rust handler (line 73-75 of `src/routes/register.rs`), not user-interpolated. We should be careful not to use `|safe` on any user-provided content. CI just passed green. Waiting for team-lead to confirm we can resume Task #6 work.
input.summary: Both points noted, will address in Task 6

routing.summary: Both points noted, will address in Task 6
